name: Helio Corp /FL/
business: Helio Corp, through its subsidiary Heliospace, is an aerospace company specializing in the design, engineering, assembly and test of space flight qualified hardware, and provides systems engineering, modeling, analysis, integration and test services to government, commercial, private and non-profit customers.

exposures.sentence: Heliospace is an aerospace company specializing in the design, engineering, assembly and test of space flight qualified hardware, providing systems engineering, modeling, analysis, integration and test services to customers in government, commercial, private and non-profit markets.
Heliospace provided deployable mechanisms and sensors as part of the Lunar Magnetotelluric Sounder (LMS) experiment.
We have pioneered distributed RF and Radar mission architectures with Department of Defense (DoD) seed funding of $109,341 that was received under an AFWERX SBIR Phase I in FY 2024.
We developed a solution to provide NASA with a high quantity of compact antennas that will deploy from a constellation of CubeSat class spacecraft on the SunRISE mission.
Helio plans to offer a modular, scalable SBSP plant based on HelioSat, our advanced deployable system that expands into large solar collection areas from small spacecraft.

catalysts.sentence: The mission is expected to launch in the summer of 2026, and is designed to image solar eruptions that impact space weather at the Earth causing satellite and communications disruptions.

catalysts.event: Optical prototype demonstrators by end of 2027
catalysts.sentence: Optical payload development is also progressing with our contributions to the telescope design for the NASA UVEX mission, with follow-on hardware work in 2025 and our own prototype demonstrators by the end of 2027.
Mission development is expected to start in FY 2026 and launch in mid-2028.

catalysts.event: Initial in-space SBSP system demonstration by 2030
catalysts.sentence: Helio plans to demonstrate an initial in-space SBSP system by 2030, followed by larger capacity in the following decade.

tamClaims.claim: Global space marketplace projected to grow from $350 billion to over $1 trillion by 2040
tamClaims.sentence: The global space marketplace is projected to grow from $350 billion today to over $1 trillion by 2040 (Morgan Stanley, Citi forecast, 2021-2022).

tamClaims.claim: Satellite System Manufacturing market size per year from 2025-2030: Science & Technology $2.3B, Earth Observation $750M, Space Domain Awareness $5.2B, In-Orbit Services $1.5B
tamClaims.sentence: Our target market, Satellite System Manufacturing, is expected to have an average market size per year of $2.3 billion for Science & Technology, $750 million for Earth Observation, $5.2 billion for Space Domain Awareness and $1.5 billion for In-Orbit Services from 2025 through 2030 (according to our estimates based on Analysys Mason Ltd., Q4 2022 projections).

tamClaims.claim: Global electricity supply market nearly $2 trillion in 2025, expanding to $3.7 trillion by 2035
tamClaims.sentence: The global market opportunity for electricity supply is substantial, nearly USD $2 trillion in 2025, expanding to $3.7 trillion by 2035.
